cot_content:
Okay, let me think about how to tell right and left earbuds apart without looking.

The question is about identifying left and right earbuds. The options are to tie a knot at the connecting end or the speaker end of the left earbud. 

I remember that in some cases, people use knots or other markings to distinguish between left and right earbuds. The idea is that when you put them in your ears, the knot would be on a specific side. But which end should the knot be tied?

If I tie a knot at the connecting end (the part that connects to the phone or device), then when I insert the earbuds, the knot would be on the side coming from the left earbud. But if I tie the knot at the speaker end (the part that goes into the ear), the knot would be inside the ear canal, which might be uncomfortable or not noticeable when you're using them. 

So the better approach is likely to tie a knot at the connecting end of the left earbud. That way, when you plug the earbuds in, you can feel the knot on the left side of the cord, helping you distinguish which one is left and which is right without looking. 

Therefore, option A seems correct.
label: minority
